name: Lambda Labs Cloud API
what_it_does: Lambda Labs Cloud provides on-demand and reserved NVIDIA GPU instances (A10, A100, H100) optimized for deep learning, accessible via a REST API for launching, managing, and terminating instances programmatically.
use_cases: Launch on-demand A100 or H100 instances via API for large-scale model fine-tuning jobs without manual portal interaction
Automate multi-node distributed training clusters by creating multiple instances in the same region and coordinating via shared filesystems
Attach persistent storage volumes to instances via API to preserve training checkpoints and datasets across instance lifecycles
Build CI/CD pipelines that spin up GPU instances, run evaluation benchmarks, and terminate them on completion
Use the instances API to monitor running jobs and implement auto-termination based on training metrics to cap costs
not_for: Workloads requiring Kubernetes-native GPU orchestration or container scheduling at scale
Teams needing serverless or autoscaling inference endpoints with sub-second cold starts
Applications requiring managed ML experiment tracking, hyperparameter search, or MLOps tooling
best_when: You need clean, well-priced NVIDIA GPU instances with a simple REST API for training runs and can manage your own software stack on top.
avoid_when: You need managed inference endpoints, autoscaling, or Kubernetes-based orchestration rather than raw VM access.

auth.notes: API key sent as HTTP Basic Auth username (password field left blank) or as Bearer token. Single key per account with no scope differentiation.

pricing.paid_tiers: On-demand: A10 (24GB) ~$0.75/hr, A100 40GB ~$1.29/hr, A100 80GB ~$1.99/hr, H100 80GB PCIe ~$2.49/hr, H100 SXM5 ~$3.29/hr. Multi-GPU configs scale linearly. Reserved instances available at ~15-25% discount.
pricing.notes: Prepaid billing with auto-recharge. On-demand availability varies by region and GPU type — H100s are frequently sold out. Reserved instances require upfront commitment.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented; in practice throttling occurs at high polling rates. Instance launch can take 1-5 minutes.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with machine-readable error codes and human-readable messages. Instance state transitions are clearly documented. OpenAPI spec is available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Instance launches are not idempotent — duplicate calls create duplicate instances. SSH key and filesystem operations are idempotent on re-add. No request deduplication tokens.
known_agent_gotchas: On-demand H100/A100 instances are frequently unavailable; agents must handle 'insufficient capacity' errors and implement region-fallback or retry logic
Instance status transitions (booting → active) are asynchronous and SSH readiness lags behind API 'active' status by several minutes
API does not expose instance CPU/memory metrics — agents needing utilization data must SSH in or install external monitoring
Persistent filesystems are region-locked; agents that launch instances across regions cannot share the same filesystem mount
Deleting an instance while jobs are still running has no confirmation or grace period — agent workflows must verify job completion before termination

security_score_components.security_notes: Dedicated datacenter hardware with NVIDIA-certified infrastructure. SOC 2 Type II compliance. API key is a single credential with full account access and no scope restrictions. SSH key management through API is clean.
